model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: This is close on the numbers, but Kentucky’s defensive edge is the separator: KenPom has the Wildcats notably better on AdjD (99.1 vs. Santa Clara 104.3), which tends to be the more stable advantage in a one-game setting. Santa Clara’s offense is legitimately dangerous (top-25 AdjO on KenPom and top-15 on Torvik), giving the 10-seed a very real upset path if it gets hot from three and Kentucky can’t turn stops into transition points. Still, with overall quality roughly comparable and no clear availability red flags surfaced in the quick scan, I’ll side with Kentucky’s higher defensive floor and SEC-tested physicality.

reasoningStep.title: Midwest 7/10 — Kentucky vs Santa Clara pick
reasoningStep.summary: Pick Kentucky in a near coin-flip, leaning on the Wildcats’ stronger defense to blunt Santa Clara’s elite offense.
evidence: KenPom: Kentucky #28 (AdjO 120.5 #39, AdjD 99.1, AdjEM +21.47) vs Santa Clara #35 (AdjO 123.7 #23, AdjD 104.3, AdjEM +19.42).
Torvik: Kentucky #32 (AdjO rank 40, AdjD rank 32) vs Santa Clara #28 (AdjO rank 15, AdjD rank 76).

Matchup logic: Santa Clara’s upset path is shot-making/3-point variance behind an elite offense; Kentucky’s path is limiting efficiency with the stronger defense and converting stops into easier points.
Availability/news scan: no clearly verified, matchup-flipping injury/availability item surfaced in the quick ESPN/web scan results returned, so the pick leans on baseline efficiency and matchup fit.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
